diagrams.title: Waterfall Model Flow
diagrams.description: A linear sequential diagram showing the five main phases of the Waterfall model: Requirements, Design, Implementation, Verification, and Maintenance. Each phase flows into the next with directional arrows, demonstrating the strict sequential nature where one phase must complete before the next begins.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the sequential, linear nature of the Waterfall model and how each phase must be completed before moving to the next, illustrating the 'cascade' flow that gives this model its name.

diagrams.title: Incremental Model Development
diagrams.description: A diagram showing multiple increments being developed in parallel or sequence. Each increment represents a working software version that builds upon the previous one, with the final increment combining all previous increments into the complete system.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how the Incremental model breaks down software development into smaller, manageable increments where each delivers a working product portion, allowing for early delivery and feedback integration.

diagrams.title: Waterfall vs Incremental Timeline Comparison
diagrams.description: A side-by-side timeline comparison showing how Waterfall completes all phases sequentially before delivery, while Incremental delivers multiple partial releases throughout development.
diagrams.purpose: Contrasts the two models by showing when deliverables are produced, helping understand why Incremental allows earlier user feedback and how it differs from Waterfall's single delivery at project end.
